next_action: Developer (IMPL mode, uat-only tier) scaffolds Playwright, runs live DOM discovery against https://m4to.com, writes E2E tests per the research plan, gets a green run. Then reviewer, then UAT with the human.
handoff_summary: TASK-004 (Playwright E2E suite for m4to.com) in progress. Research complete: site is a live client-side-rendered SPA titled 'Rafael Matovello - Multiverse Portfolio'; WebFetch sees only an empty shell, so selectors must come from a live JS-executing discovery pass. Plan: landing-page load test, nav/link flows, button interactions, contact form asserted up to the submit boundary only (intercept network if it posts to a real backend). Low worker count against the live site. Developer spawn is next.
open_questions: Are /about, /contact real routes or in-page anchors? (catch-all SPA fallback returns identical shell for all paths — check HTTP status codes during discovery)
Does the contact form post to a live backend (must intercept) or is it mailto:/client-only?

decisions.decision: Deprioritize seeded placeholder tickets (TASK-001..003) at human direction; real work item is TASK-004 Playwright tests for m4to.com.
Contact-form tests are boundary-only: never trigger a real submission; use route interception if the form posts to a backend.

subagent_results.summary: m4to.com is live: 'Rafael Matovello - Multiverse Portfolio', a client-side-rendered SPA. All paths (incl. /robots.txt) return an identical minimal HTML shell — WebFetch cannot see the DOM, so no selectors could be pre-enumerated; developer must run a live Playwright discovery pass first. Test plan: (1) landing load — title /Rafael Matovello/i + hydrated heading visible; (2) nav links via getByRole with destination-visibility assertions (routes may be in-page anchors); (3) buttons incl. popup handling for target=_blank; (4) contact form filled with obvious test data, asserted up to submit boundary only — intercept with page.route() if it posts to a real service. Hazards: hydration waits needed (prefer auto-retrying toBeVisible over networkidle-only), possible consent banner, catch-all fallback masking 404s, live-site rate limits (run workers:1-2). Config: baseURL https://m4to.com, generous timeouts. Site not indexed anywhere; no Wayback snapshot available.
